CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF MERCHANTS EXCHANGE BUILDING LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 2.0 DAYS

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SUNG CHO MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. THE COURT HAS CONSIDERED THE SUPPLEMENTAL FILINGS. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS UNTIMELY. UNDER CCP 473(B), A MOTION TO SET ASIDE MUST BE MADE WITHIN SIX MONTHS OF THE ENTRY OF DEFAULT. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. CCP 473(B) AND 473(C)(1)(A) ONLY PROVIDE FOR SANCTIONS WHEN RELIEF FROM DEFAULT IS GRANTED. THIS RULING IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT'S OTHER REMEDIES. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Nov-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MENGMENG ZHANG, ESQ., 415.979.0480, FOR PLAINTIFF MERCHANTS EXCHANGE BUILDING LLC; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling RE: DEFENDANT SUNG CHO MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. The Court has considered the supplemental filings. Defendant's motion to set aside default is denied. Defendant's motion is untimely. Under CCP 473(b), a motion to set aside must be made within six months of the entry of default. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. CCP 473(b) and 473(c)(1)(A) only provide for sanctions when relief from default is granted. This ruling is without prejudice to Defendant's other remedies. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Oct-21-2013 Continued To Nov-13-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SUNG CHO'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND.

Appearances: Mengmeng Zhang of Bogaards Davis LLP (415.979.0480) for plaintiff and responding party; H. Kenneth Ahn (415.474.8160) appearing for defendant and moving party.

Argued and the Court modifies its ruling as follows: The matter is continued to November 13, 2013. Defendant to file and serve an additional declaration by October 31, 2013. Plaintiff may file and serve a supplemental reply not to exceed 5 pages by November 8, 2013. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR #6185 (302/MJM)
